Klasse GamePersistenceManager

java.lang.Object
persistance.GamePersistenceManager
public class GamePersistenceManager extends Object
Manages the persistence of game states by saving and loading game data to and from JSON files.

This class provides methods to create a serializable representation of the current game state, save it to a file, and restore it again.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • GamePersistenceManager

      public GamePersistenceManager()

  • Methodendetails

    • createGameState

      public GameStateData createGameState(PlayerEntity white, PlayerEntity black, PlayerEntity current, History history, int noMillTurnCounter, int timePerTurn, int timeLeft, String mode)
      Creates a GameStateData object containing all necessary information to restore the current game state. This includes player data, move history, and game settings.

      The GameStateData can be serialized into JSON and saved to a file.

      This method is used when saving the current game state, e.g. before exiting the application or when the user chooses to save the game.

      Parameter:
      white - The white player.
      black - The black player
      current - The current player.
      history - The history containing the undo- and redo-stacks.
      noMillTurnCounter - The amount of turns no mills where formed.
      timePerTurn - The time each player has to perform a move.
      timeLeft - The time the current player has left to perform a move.
      mode - The current mode defining if there is a CPU or not.
      Gibt zurück:
      The game state containing the current game.
      Siehe auch:
      • toPlayerData(PlayerEntity)
      • toMoveData(Move)

    • saveGameState

      public void saveGameState(GameStateData gameState, File targetFile)
      Saves the current game state to a JSON-File.

      This method serializes the GameStateData created by createGameState(PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, History, int, int, int, String) and writes it to the specified target file.

      If the file already exists, it will be overwritten.

      Parameter:
      gameState - The GameStateData containing the current game state.
      targetFile - The target file to which the game state should be saved.
      Löst aus:
      com.google.gson.JsonIOException - if the data could not be written into a JSON-File.

    • toPlayerData

      private PlayerData toPlayerData(PlayerEntity player)
      Helpermethod for createGameState(PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, History, int, int, int, String).

      Converts a PlayerEntity to its corresponding serializable PlayerData.

      Parameter:
      player - The player entity to convert.
      Gibt zurück:
      A PlayerData object containing serializable fields.

    • toMoveData

      private MoveData toMoveData(Move move)
      Helpermethod for createGameState(PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, PlayerEntity, History, int, int, int, String).

      Converts a Move to its corresponding serializable MoveData.

      Parameter:
      move - The move to convert.
      Gibt zurück:
      A MoveData object containing serializable fields.

    • createBoardSnapshot

      private Map<Integer,Integer> createBoardSnapshot(Map<Integer,Node> board)
      Helpermethod for toMoveData(Move).

      Creates a simplified board snapshot from a full board state.

      • 0 = empty
      • 1 = white
      • 2 = black
      Parameter:
      board - The full board state as a map of nodes.
      Gibt zurück:
      A simplified map of node IDs to owner codes.

    • restoreGameState

      public GameStateData restoreGameState(File file)
      Loads a saved game state from a JSON-File and restores it.
      Parameter:
      file - The JSON file containing the saved game state.
      Gibt zurück:
      The GameStateData containing the saved game state.
      Löst aus:
      com.google.gson.JsonIOException - if the data could not be read from a JSON-File.

    • fromPlayerData

      public PlayerEntity fromPlayerData(PlayerData data, PlayerEntity opponentIfCPU, boolean placeStones)
      Reconstructs a PlayerEntity from its saved PlayerData.

      This method is used when loading a game state to restore the players with their names, phases, and stones.

      Parameter:
      data - The PlayerData containing saved state.
      opponentIfCPU - The opponent of the CPUPlayer. Can be null for Player.
      Gibt zurück:
      A PlayerEntity with restored name, phase, and stones.

    • fromMoveData

      public Move fromMoveData(MoveData data, PlayerEntity loadedWhite, PlayerEntity loadedBlack)
      Reconstructs a Move from its saved MoveData.

      This method is used when loading a game state to restore the move history with the correct board state and placed stones.

      Parameter:
      data - The MoveData containing saved move state.
      loadedWhite - The PlayerEntity representing the white player.
      loadedBlack - The PlayerEntity representing the black player.
      Gibt zurück:
      A Move object with restored board state and placed stones.

    • fromPhaseData

      private GamePhase fromPhaseData(String phase, PlayerEntity player)
